<> To replace one URL with another within a string in PHP you can use the `str_replace` function. This function allows you to search for a specific string and replace it with another. Here is an example of how to do it: ```php $string = "New post on the site http://stackoverflow.com/xyz1

"; $old_url = "http://stackoverflow.com/xyz1"; $new_url = "http://yahoo.com/abc1"; $replaced_string = str_replace($old_url, $new_url, $string); echo $replaced_string; ``` This code will output: ``` New post on the site http://yahoo.com/abc1

``` The `str_replace` function works for repeating strings as well. It will replace every occurrence of the old URL with the new one in the given string. A palindrome is a word that reads the same forwards and backwards. An example of a palindrome is "madam" or "racecar". These words are symmetrical and maintain their form when reversed. Palindromes can be found in many languages and are often used in puzzles and word games. They are also used in various fields such as computer science and mathematics to demonstrate symmetry and pattern recognition.
